query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "perception or prejudice: can mllms go beyond first impressions of pers" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it empirically measures the same core construct (MLLM bias and prejudice) and the specific mechanism of "first impressions" (via adversarial bias elicitation and jailbreaks) that the user's referenced work investigates, providing essential baseline data on model robustness to stereotyping.
